title: Methods and agricultural compositions for preventing or controlling plant diseases
abstract: The disclosure provides a method of treating, preventing, and/or controlling plant diseases by applying the agricultural composition comprising a bacterial isolate belonging to Paenibacillus or Bacillus. Also, the disclosure provides a method of enhancing disease resistance of a plant seed by applying the agricultural composition to the plant seed, and an agricultural composition comprising the bacterial isolate.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of controlling plant disease, comprising administering an effective amount of an agricultural composition to a plant, the agricultural composition comprising a bacterial isolate of Paenibacillus or Bacillus, or a mutant thereof. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the bacterial isolate comprises MS1479, MS2379, MS2414, MS2820, MS0633, MS2335, MS2652, MS2658, MS2681, MS2697, MS2712 or combination thereof. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the bacterial isolate is MS2379 or MS2414. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the agricultural composition is administered to a plant seed, an immature seedling, and/or a tissue in the plant. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the plant disease comprises a fungal disease, a bacterial disease, a viral disease, or a parasitic disease. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the plant disease is caused by a pathogen, wherein the pathogen comprises a fungus, an oomycete, a bacterium, a virus, a viroid, a virus-like organism, a phytoplasma, a protist, a nematode, a parasitic plant, or combination thereof. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the plant disease is a fungal disease. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the fungal disease comprises white blister, downy mildews, powdery mildews, clubroot, sclerotinia rot, fusarium wilts and rots, botrytis rots, anthracnose, rhizoctonia rots, damping-off, cavity spot, tuber diseases, rusts, black root rot, target spot, aphanomyces root rot, ascochyta collar rot, gummy stem blight, alternaria leaf spot, black leg, ring spot, late blight, cercospora, leaf blight, septoria spot, leaf blight, or combination thereof. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the fungal disease is caused by Macrophomina phaseolina, Fusarium virguliforme, Rhizoctonia solani, Botrytis cinerea, Pythium ultimum, and Pythium irregulare. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the agricultural composition further comprises a bio-control formulation, wherein the bio-control formulation comprises a microbe. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the agricultural composition further comprises an insecticide, a nematicide, an acaricide, a fungicide, a bactericides, an herbicide, a plant growth regulator, a spreader, a fertilizer, a microbial material, or a soil amendment. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the fungicide comprises an active ingredient from Serenade Â® , Satori Â® , Double Nickel Â® , LifeGard Â® , Xanthion Â® A, or Subtilex Â® . 13. The method of claim 10, wherein the colony forming unit (cfu) ratio of the bacterial isolate to the microbe in the bio-control formulation is in a range of from 1,000: 1 to 1 : 1,000, 100: 1 to 1 : 100, 50: 1 to 1 :50, or 10: 1 to 1 : 10. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the cfu ratio is in a range of from 100: 1 to 1 : 1. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the cfu ratio is in a range of from 50: 1 to 10: 1. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the concentration of the bacterial isolate is at least 1.3xl0 5 cfu/ml, 1.3xl0 6 cfu/ml, 1.3xl0 7 cfu/ml, 1.3xl0 8 cfu/ml, 1.3xl0 9 cfu/ml, or 1.3xl0 10 cfu/ml. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the concentration of the bacterial isolate is from lxlO 5 cfu/ml to lxlO 10 cfu/ml, from lxlO 6 cfu/ml to 5xl0 9 cfu/ml, from lxlO 7 cfu/ml to lxlO 9 cfu/ml, or from 5xl0 7 cfu/ml to 5xl0 8 cfu/ml. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17 , further comprising a fermentation process of a culture comprising the bacterial isolate, wherein the fermentation process comprises: (1) inoculating the bacterial isolate in a seed medium, and (2) expanding the culture with a production medium. 19.
